skill.name: agent-browser
skill.description: Browser automation for AI agents via inference.sh. Navigate web pages, interact with elements using @e refs, take screenshots, record video. Capabilities: web scraping, form filling, clicking, typing, drag-drop, file upload, JavaScript execution. Use for: web automation, data extraction, testing, agent browsing, research. Triggers: browser, web automation, scrape, navigate, click, fill form, screenshot, browse web, playwright, headless browser, web agent, surf internet, record video
skill.summary: Automate web browsers for AI agents with Playwright-based navigation, element interaction, and session management via inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 606 patterns across 10 files (2313 lines). Most findings are false positives: external_commands are documentation examples showing CLI usage, network patterns are example URLs, filesystem patterns are markdown relative links. The skill legitimately requires Bash permissions for inference.sh CLI, network access for browser automation, and filesystem access for screenshots/videos. No malicious intent detected. Users should understand the powerful capabilities this skill provides.

medium_findings.title: Extensive External Command Usage
medium_findings.description: The skill uses Bash(infsh *) permission extensively for browser automation via inference.sh CLI. While this is the intended functionality, users should understand this grants significant system access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Explicit Bash permission declared in skill header with wildcard pattern allowing all infsh commands.

medium_findings.title: Browser Credential and Cookie Access
medium_findings.description: The skill can access browser cookies and storage via JavaScript execution. This is required for authenticated browsing but could expose session tokens if misused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation explicitly shows cookie extraction via document.cookie and describes storage access as core functionality.

medium_findings.title: Screen Capture and Video Recording
medium_findings.description: The skill can capture screenshots and record video of browser sessions. This capability could capture sensitive information displayed during automation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Screenshot and video recording are documented core features with dedicated reference documentation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Example URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation contains example URLs (example.com, inference.sh). These are documentation examples, not malicious endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are standard example domains and the legitimate inference.sh service URL in documentation context.

low_findings.title: SOCKS Proxy Configuration Support
low_findings.description: The skill supports SOCKS proxy configuration for browser traffic routing. This is a legitimate privacy/networking feature.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Proxy support is documented as an optional configuration feature with clear use cases for privacy and testing.

content.user_title: Automate Web Browsers for AI Agents
content.value_statement: AI agents need to interact with websites but lack native browser access. This skill provides Playwright-based browser automation through inference.sh, enabling navigation, element interaction, screenshots, and video recording with session management for complex multi-step workflows.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to any URL and capture page state with interactive element references
Click, fill, drag, select, and interact with web elements using @e reference system
Manage persistent browser sessions with cookies and localStorage for authenticated browsing
Capture screenshots and record video of browser sessions
Execute custom JavaScript on pages and extract results
Support proxy configurations including HTTP and SOCKS proxies
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication (infsh login)
Sessions do not persist across server restarts and must be explicitly closed
Cannot bypass CAPTCHA or anti-bot protections on websites
Video recordings are only available until the session is closed

use_cases.title: Web Scraping and Data Extraction
use_cases.description: Extract structured data from websites that require JavaScript rendering or user interaction, such as dashboards, dynamic content, or sites without public APIs.

use_cases.title: Automated Testing and QA
use_cases.description: Test web applications by automating user workflows, form submissions, navigation paths, and visual verification through screenshots.

use_cases.title: Agent Research and Information Gathering
use_cases.description: Enable AI agents to browse websites, read documentation, check product availability, monitor prices, or gather information from web sources.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Page Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Open a browser session and navigate to https://example.com. Show me what interactive elements are available on the page.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Simple navigation to explore a website

prompt_templates.title: Form Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a browser session to fill out the contact form at https://example.com/contact. Find the name, email, and message fields, fill them with test data, and submit the form. Take a screenshot after submission.

prompt_templates.title: Authenticated Data Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a recorded browser session. Log into https://app.example.com using credentials from environment variables. Navigate to the dashboard, extract all metrics displayed in the table, and close the session returning the video.

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Page Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a browser session with a SOCKS proxy. Search for products on an e-commerce site, filter by price range, open the first 3 product pages in sequence, extract product details from each, and compile a comparison summary. Record the entire workflow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert - Proxy usage with complex multi-page automation

output_examples.input: Navigate to example.com and show available elements
output_examples.output: Session created: sess_abc123. Page loaded successfully. Available elements: @e1 [h1] 'Example Domain', @e2 [p] 'This domain is for use in documentation', @e3 [a] 'Learn more' href='https://iana.org/domains/example'
Element @e1 filled with 'test@example.com'. Element @e2 filled with 'password'. Element @e3 clicked. Page navigated to /dashboard. Session remains active.
Screenshot captured: screenshot_20260312_082145.png (1920x1080). Image saved to /tmp/browser-sessions/sess_abc123/screenshots/. Base64 preview available in response.

best_practices: Always close sessions when done to free resources and retrieve video recordings if enabled
Use environment variables for credentials instead of hardcoding them in automation scripts
Take snapshots after any navigation or DOM-modifying action to get fresh element references
Use wait actions or explicit conditions before interacting with dynamically loaded elements
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode credentials directly in automation scripts or prompts
Do not assume element references persist after page navigation - always re-snapshot
Do not leave sessions running indefinitely - implement timeout and cleanup logic
Do not use for bypassing security measures, CAPTCHA, or unauthorized access to protected resources

faq.question: What do I need to install before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI (infsh). Run 'npx skills add inference-sh/skills@agent-tools' for installation instructions, then authenticate with 'infsh login'.

faq.question: How do element references (@e1, @e2) work?
faq.answer: When you open a page or call snapshot, the skill returns a list of interactive elements with @e references. Use these refs in interact commands like click or fill. References expire when the DOM changes.

faq.question: Can this skill handle login and authenticated sessions?
faq.answer: Yes. Sessions persist cookies and localStorage across function calls. Fill login forms, submit, and subsequent actions in that session remain authenticated. See authentication.md for OAuth and 2FA patterns.

faq.question: How does video recording work?
faq.answer: Enable video by adding 'record_video': true to the open function input. The video is recorded throughout the session and returned when you call the close function. Videos are only available until the session closes.

faq.question: Can I use proxies with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Add 'proxy': {'server': 'http://proxy.example.com:8080'} or SOCKS with 'proxy': {'server': 'socks5://proxy.example.com:1080'} to the open function input. See proxy-support.md for details.

faq.question: What happens if I forget to close a session?
faq.answer: Sessions consume server resources. While there may be automatic cleanup, always explicitly call close when done. Unclosed sessions may timeout but this is not guaranteed.
